lines.en_lit: his eye on his cup, those with whom relations are forbidden seem to him like level [mishor] And one said: Whoever casts his eye on his cup, the whole world seems to him like level [mishor] ground.

“If there is care in a man’s heart, let him quash it [yashḥena]” Rabbi Ami and Rabbi Asi One said: He should forcefully push it [yasḥena] out of his mind. And one said: he should tell [yesiḥena] others his

“And dust shall be the serpent’s food” Rabbi Ami and Rabbi Asi One said: Even if eats all the delicacies in the world, they will taste like dust. And one said: Even if it eats all the delicacies in the world, its mind is unsettled until it eats dust.

it was taught Rabbi Yosei said: Come and see that the attribute of the Holy One, Blessed be He, is different than the attribute of flesh and blood. The attribute of flesh and blood provoke another harasses him his life, but the Holy One, Blessed be He, does not this He cursed the serpent the serpent goes up to the roof its food is with it, it comes down its food is with it.
He cursed Canaan He eats what his master eats, and drinks what his master drinks, He cursed the woman everyone pursues her He cursed the land everyone is sustained from it.

Rav and Shmuel “We remember the fish which we ate in Egypt for nothing” One said: fish. And one said: relations The one who said fish as it is written “which we ate.” And the one who said forbidden sexual relations as it is written “for nothing.”

according to the one who said relations, but written “which we ate”? employed a euphemistic expression. as it is written: she eats, and wipes her mouth, and says I have done no wickedness” And according to the one who said fish, what “for nothing”? brought the fish from ownerless property, The Master said: When the Jews drew water the Holy One, Blessed be He, prepared little fish for them in the water. into their jugs.

Granted, according to the one who said fish but not promiscuous forbidden relations this is what is written “A garden enclosed is my sister the bride; However, according to the one who said forbidden sexual relations, what “a sealed spring” not promiscuous those who were forbidden

Granted, according to the one who said forbidden sexual relations, this is as it is written: “And Moses heard the people weeping for their families” with regard to the issue of their families, because it became prohibited for them to cohabit with them. But according to the one who says fish what “weeping for their families” Both this and that happened.

the cucumbers, and the melons, Rabbi Ami and Rabbi Asi One said: They tasted the flavor of all types of food in the manna, they could not taste the tastes of these five foods And one said: They tasted the flavor of all types of food, their textures. their flavor but not their texture.

“And it was white [lavan] like coriander seed; and its flavor Rabbi Asi said: round like coriander but white like a pearl. This was also taught Coriander [gad] because it is similar to flax seeds on their stalks,

Others say: coriander [gad] because it is similar to a tale [haggada], which draws a person’s heart like water, It was taught another gad? Because it told [maggid] the Jewish people if nine first or if seven second
white because it whitened Israel’s sins.

it was taught Rabbi Yosei says: Just like the prophet would tell the Jewish people what was in the holes and what was in the cracks so too, the manna clarified for Israel what was in the holes and what was in the cracks. How so? two came before Moses for a judgment, one saying: You stole my slave, and one saying: you sold him to me, Moses say to them: In the morning a judgment. If on the following day found his omer in his first master’s house, it would be clear that he was stolen, And if on the following day he found his omer in his second master’s house, it would be clear that he had been sold.

a man and a woman came to Moses for a judgment, he saying: She sinned against me, and she saying: He sinned against me Moses say to them: In the morning there will be a judgment. The following day, if her omer was found in her husband’s house, it would be clear that she sinned against him. If her omer was found in her father’s house, it would be clear that he sinned against her.

It is written: “And when the dew fell upon the camp in the night, the manna fell upon it” And it is written: “And the people shall go out and gather And it is written: “The people went about and gathered it” How these righteous, fell at the opening of their homes. average people went out and gathered The wicked to go about to gather.
it is written “bread” and it is written “cakes” and it is written “and ground it How these righteous, bread; average people, cakes; wicked ground it

“Or beat it in a mortar” Rabbi Yehuda said Rav said, and some say Rabbi Ḥama, son of Rabbi Ḥanina: This teaches that women’s perfumes fell for the Jewish people with the manna an item beaten in a mortar. “And cooked it in a pot” Rabbi Ḥama said: This teaches that cooking spices fell for the Jewish people with the manna.

“And they brought yet to him free-will offerings every morning” What “every morning”? Rabbi Shmuel bar Naḥmani said Rabbi Yonatan said: from that which fell every morning This teaches that pearls and precious stones fell for the Israelites with the manna. “And the rulers [nesi’im] brought the onyx [shoham] stones” taught nesi’im actual As it states: “As clouds [nesi’im] and wind without rain

“And its taste was as the taste of a cake [shad] baked with oil [hashamen]” Rabbi Abbahu said: Just as a baby tastes different flavors from the breast, so too with the manna, every time that the Jewish people ate they found in it many flavors, There are those who say literally a demon. Just as a demon changes into different forms so too, the manna changed into different flavors.

“And Moses said: This shall be, when the Lord will give you in the evening meat to eat, and in the morning bread to the full” taught in the name of Rabbi Yehoshua ben Korḥa: The meat that the Jewish people asked for inappropriately, given to them inappropriately,
